title: The Night I Understood the Loop

story.opening: <break time="500ms"/>There was a night - <break time="400ms"/>maybe month eight of the insomnia - <break time="350ms"/>when I finally understood what was happening.
story.body: <break time="450ms"/>I was lying there. <break time="400ms"/>Couldn't sleep. <break time="350ms"/>And I was thinking: <break time="300ms"/>'I need to sleep. <break time="250ms"/>I have work tomorrow. <break time="200ms"/>If I don't sleep I'll be useless. <break time="150ms"/>Why can't I sleep? <break time="100ms"/>What's wrong with me?' <break time="600ms"/>And I felt my heart rate go up. <break time="500ms"/>My body getting more tense. <break time="400ms"/>And I realized: <break time="350ms"/>the anxiety about not sleeping <break time="300ms"/>was making sleep impossible. <break time="600ms"/>The more I tried, the worse it got. <break time="500ms"/>The loop was feeding itself.
story.reflection: <break time="600ms"/>Breaking that loop was the hardest thing. <break time="500ms"/>I had to stop trying to sleep. <break time="400ms"/>To let go of the outcome. <break time="350ms"/>To accept that I might not sleep - <break time="300ms"/>and be okay with that. <break time="600ms"/>Only then did sleep start to come back. <break time="500ms"/>It's counterintuitive. <break time="400ms"/>And so hard when you're in it. <break time="350ms"/>But that's how the loop breaks.

when_to_share: When someone is caught in anxiety about not sleeping
